body.title: Add revision/updated affordance to palace rules (versioned memory)
body.result: Every theigors/rules/* palace node carries 'revision: 2026-04-21 — initial versioned tag' line. Pattern established: future rule edits append new revision lines. 16 nodes updated (rules + ticket_design_checks children). Commit 706f3143.
body.description:
Rules look immutable but aren't. Need an evolvability affordance so rules are visibly 'current best version, improvable' — not proclamations. Akien 2026-04-21: 'a versioned memory is the answer there too.'

**Shape:** each palace rule row gets a `revision:` line in its content (e.g. 'revision: 2026-04-21 — initial'). Updated each time the rule changes. Use versioned memory pattern: content stores mini-changelog inline. If memory_palace schema already supports a version_history field, use it; otherwise inline YAML block is sufficient for now.

**Affected files:** palace rows under `theigors/rules/*` (content edits for revision: affordance). `lab/claudecode/palace_sync.py` — if touched to support versioned sync, update its docstring to reflect versioned content handling. clan.memory_palace table (Postgres, existing — no schema migration needed unless version_history column is added, in which case note migration here).

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres only, clan.memory_palace existing); no-new-memory-schemas (versioning goes into content YAML, not a new table); docs-in-code: if palace_sync.py is touched, update its docstring; test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: add revision: line to each existing theigors/rules/* node. Establish the pattern.
- Out: versioning non-rule palace nodes. Out: automated version-history rollup UI.

**Test plan:** no new tests. Verification: `SELECT content FROM memory_palace WHERE path LIKE 'theigors/rules/%'` — every row contains 'revision:' line. If schema migration: INSERT + SELECT round-trip confirms column.
